nodes.label: P2.5 — multi-block: GD *between* shallow SCFF
nodes.description: Fixed total SCFF depth=8, varying GD-checkpoint cadence N in {1,2,4,8} blocks x mode {read (boost block readouts, ensemble) vs write (re-inject GD hidden rep into next SCFF block)}. Per-block SCFF = P2.1 healthy cell.

nodes.label: read vs write fork
nodes.description: read N=8: 0.302 (+0.010 vs pure-SCFF 0.292, disjoint-IQR, 5/5) at 38.5k backward weights. write N=8: 0.205 (-0.087, monotone decline with more checkpoints) at same backward cost. pure-GD ceiling: 0.354 at 230k backward weights.

nodes.label: P2.5 verdict: read earns depth marginally+cheaply; write fails decisively
nodes.description: read reaches ~85% of pure-GD accuracy (0.302/0.354) at ~17% of its backward cost (38k/230k) — a ~6x backward saving. write is actively harmful because the GD-realign hidden is class-collapsed; running SCFF on it forces the next block to re-cluster a low-information representation, throwing away rich raw features.

nodes.label: Decision: depth arc closed constructively -> route to P2.6 (substrate filter + continual veto)
nodes.description: The recipe = cheap boosted ensembles of shallow SCFF blocks (read), GD as readout not stream-rewriter. Cadence k is a cost knob (few blocks suffice, saturates ~2-4). Must now verify this recipe is online-feasible and preserves the Phase-1 continual win.
